title: AI Is Replacing Junior Jobs Faster Than Expected, Says New CEO Survey
description: Recent findings from a global survey indicate a significant shift in hiring practices among CEOs, as many are moving away from recruiting entry-level employees due to the increasing capabilities of artificial intelligence in performing routine tasks. , Technology & Science, Times Now
content:
Artificial intelligence is reshaping hiring practices, with a recent survey indicating that many CEOs are prioritising experienced professionals over entry-level candidates. (AI Image)

title: More than 100 drones on standby for wildfire season across Greece
description: The Hellenic Fire Service and Civil Protection of Greece are deploying unmanned aerial vehicles equipped with thermal cameras and artificial intelligence for the immediate monitoring, detection, and management of forest fires
content:
More than 100 drones distributed across all regions of Greece will be available to the Fire Service and Civil Protection during this year’s wildfire season, as part of the country’s forest surveillance mechanism.

title: As electric bills rise, some states are focusing on the growing profits of utilities
description: The artificial intelligence boom is leading to fights in some states over growing utility profits. Governors, attorneys general and others are protesting rising electricity bills and say cash-strapped residents are stuck in a broken system. Officials in over a half-dozen states are going to new lengths to try to block rate increases proposed by utilities. Some are pressing utilities to completely change their model for financing major system upgrades. The push comes during a midterm election year in which “affordability” is the leading theme in Democrats’ attempts to loosen Republicans’ control of Washington.
